text: Risk management is an essential skill in both business and daily life. It starts with identifying risks, which means looking ahead to see what could go wrong. Once potential problems are spotted, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their likelihood or impact. For example, a company might install fire alarms to prevent damage. However, not all risks can be avoided. That is why contingency plans are important. These are backup actions you take if something does happen, like having extra suppliers in case one fails. Finally, you must constantly monitor the situation, because risks can change over time. By following these steps, people and organizations can feel more prepared and secure.
